result:
Here's a summary of the fix:

## Root Cause

The issue is that `text/csv` — the standard IANA MIME type for CSV files — was not recognized by AnkiDroid. Android's `MimeTypeMap.getMimeTypeFromExtension("csv")` returns `"text/csv"`, which is what most file managers and content providers will report for `.csv` files. However, AnkiDroid only checked for the non-standard `"text/comma-separated-values"` MIME type.

This meant:
1. When a user opened a CSV file from a file manager, Android would resolve the MIME type to `text/csv`, which fell through to `LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT` instead of `LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT`. The FILE_IMPORT path only accepts `.apkg` and `.colpkg` files, so the import failed.
2. When a content provider (like Gmail or Google Drive) provided a CSV file with `text/csv` MIME type, Android wouldn't even offer AnkiDroid as an app to handle it, since the manifest's MIME type intent filter didn't include `text/csv`.

## Changes Made

### 1. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/IntentHandler.kt` (line 338)
Added `mimeType == "text/csv"` to the `TEXT_IMPORT` launch type check in `getLaunchType()`, so CSV files with the standard MIME type are correctly routed to the CSV import flow.

### 2. `AnkiDroid/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml` (line 244)
Added `<data android:mimeType="text/csv"/>` to the MIME type intent filter, so Android will offer AnkiDroid as a handler for files with `text/csv` MIME type from content providers that don't expose the file extension.
